opp.name: Greencore_s2p_2026
opp.next_step: Inviting to EY event in OCtober WIll be inviting to our EY event in October This is a long play deal - we are keeping them warm until they are ready for RFP Conducted on-site discovery and demonstration meeting at the customer's offices on 15th May 2026 with Rachel and James. Rachel showed strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities. James has confirmed attendance at the Zycus procurement event next week — good opportunity to deepen the relationship in a peer environment. Rachel is impressed with what Zycus can deliver but has raised a significant concern around internal resource availability to support a procurement transformation programme. She does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project. This is the primary blocker to near-term progression. Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026. Zycus is being positioned to help her build the business case and shape the RFP — giving us a strong influence position ahead of formal process launch.
opp.ais_why:
• The SF Opportunity Description records Greencore's strategic context: 'The organization is currently undergoing a strategic transformation program called Making Business Easier (MBE), aimed at modernizing its supply chain, improving operational efficiency, and enabling data-driven decision-making across the enterprise.' Following the Bakkavor acquisition reaching ~¬£4B revenue, Greencore has 'significantly expanded its scale... increased operational complexity and accelerated the need for digital transformation.' Full Zycus AI suite in scope (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) aligned to this MBE program.
• Qualified-stage with recurring April 2026 working sessions and a scheduled 15 May 2026 meeting attended by Greencore stakeholders James Dean and Rachael Coad with Zycus team (Anthony Gray, Shriya Iyer, Parama Sarkar). BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC but the multi-party meeting cadence suggests active discovery underway. The data-driven decision-making framing from the Description aligns naturally with Merlin Insights and ANA positioning.
• Confidence is Medium because the SF Description provides a strong strategic context but the Avoma summary for this opp returned content from an unrelated account (Avoma fuzzy-match error), so we lack verbatim customer-side AI quotes. The score relies on the Description's explicit transformation language and the agentic/Merlin product mix. A confirmed AI champion or RFP issuance would move this to 8+.

items.quote: this is a long play deal - we are keeping them warm until they are ready for RFP

metrics.narrative: The business problem is vividly articulated and partially quantified by Rachael Coad on the 10 Apr 2026 discovery call: Greencore runs ~7 ERP systems, indirect spend ~GBP 650m/year with ~30% in tail and ~2,000 live indirect suppliers '80% of that in tail', and procurement 'never sees a purchase order' so controls and visibility are absent. She referenced a publicly announced GBP 80m post-acquisition synergy target ('it is really quite significant... an S2P system would really help') though she could not yet attribute a portion to procurement. Anthony Gray committed to a savings/business-case model (3-5% savings, +30% spend under management) but no Greencore-validated ROI figure exists yet. The named, quantified value case is the single biggest metrics gap and converting the GBP 80m synergy and tail-spend numbers into a Greencore-specific model is the priority move.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad (Senior Controller - Indirect Procurement, SF Decision Maker) is a strong, self-declared champion. She said she 'will be running the project' and owning the solution, is 'deeply invested', is 'a big fan', and explicitly wants Zycus to help her sell internally: 'help me bring that to life to my business so I can get the rubber stamp, get the money, get going.' She brought James Dean (Head of Procurement Excellence) as co-owner and attended the on-site demo 15 May 2026. Her advocacy is genuine but her leverage is limited: she is pre-budget, candidly noted 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet', and per the Next Step flagged she lacks internal headcount/bandwidth to run the implementation. So the champion is strong on intent but needs Zycus-supplied ammunition (business case, ROI model) and a path past the resourcing blocker. Multi-threading beyond Rachael and James to IT, finance and senior leadership is the gap.

competition.narrative: There is no head-to-head S2P competitor in the deal yet; the formal shortlist forms at the end-2026 RFP, where Zycus currently holds a shaping advantage by providing the RFP primer. The real present 'competition' is the fragmented status quo Zycus would replace (Scanmarket eProcurement, DocuSign contract repository, click spend analytics) and best-of-breed point AI tools Greencore already runs: Pactum for autonomous negotiation ('we're in our second year of having it now') and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool Rachael 'loves'. These point tools both validate Greencore's AI appetite and create a 'do nothing / keep best-of-breed' alternative to a single agentic platform. Zycus wins by proving one-data-core consolidation plus a broader agentic framework beats stitched point tools, and by owning the RFP criteria before rivals build parallel relationships.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is strong, specific and owner-confirmed by Rachael Coad (10 Apr 2026). Greencore doubled in size after acquiring Bakavol (GBP 4bn turnover) and runs fragmented, manual procurement on ~7 ERPs with standalone tools (Scanmarket for eProcurement, DocuSign as contract repository that 'doesn't tie into anything', click for spend visibility that 'works well for directs, not well for indirects'). Procurement has zero PO visibility ('I've never seen a purchase order'), no supplier risk management ('they are not managed'), uncontrolled tail spend (she cited a site buying engineering day-rate resource from a nuts-and-bolts supplier), and contracts auto-renewing because dates were unmanaged. She summarised the day-to-day as 'a lot of what we do is firefighting'. The pain owner is Rachael (indirect procurement / technology) with executive air cover from the GBP 80m synergy and 'Making Business Easier' transformation programme.

sources: Avoma discovery call 10 Apr 2026 (Anthony Gray, Rachael Coad)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in view, which is expected for a pre-RFP, pre-budget Qualified deal. No MSA/SOW, legal, procurement-channel or InfoSec discussion has occurred. Greencore works with about 5 system integrators ('we've got a whole suite behind us', 10 Apr 2026) and Anthony Gray raised whether a GSI such as EY would lead delivery, so an SI/partner will likely be involved at implementation. Visibility will be created naturally as the business case and RFP take shape; the move now is to use the RFP primer to surface the intended procurement and approval route early.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. Rachael Coad confirmed on 10 Apr 2026 she 'will be running the project' and 'looks like... will be owning the solution', but explicitly said funding is not approved: 'The business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet... part of my job is being able to create a really compelling business case that I can go to the business with and say it's an absolute no brainer, and then we can just press the button.' That places real budget authority with unnamed senior leadership above her (she referenced 'the powers that be' and 'my boss'). The deal is pre-budget-approval. The evidence shows budget authority sits in a leadership audience Rachael must convince, not yet with anyone we have met, so identifying and reaching that economic buyer is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: Rachael Coad described the process clearly on 10 Apr 2026: build a compelling business case first, then go to market ('I want to build a business case, and then we're gonna go to market... I don't want to go to market first'), issue an RFP towards end of 2026, then secure budget sign-off from senior leadership before a full team (project managers, IT, business analysts) is assembled. The call sequence shows a tight two-person buying core today (Rachael plus James Dean, Head of Procurement Excellence) with a wide implementation committee to form only post-approval. Zycus has a strong shaping position pre-RFP. The approval chain above Rachael is not yet named, and that is the key process gap to close before the RFP launches.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are emerging from the discovery and the requested demo, not yet formalised. Rachael Coad asked Zycus to run an interactive, live, customised demo on her specific use cases emphasising AI capabilities, savings-opportunity analysis, contract management and supplier risk (10 Apr 2026), and showed particular interest in the savings-opportunity agent and autonomous tail-spend consolidation ('you buy paper clips from 17 different suppliers... consolidate all of that into this one supplier'). AI/agentic capability is a clear weighted criterion: she repeatedly raised her appetite for AI ('the AI autonomous negotiation tool gets mentioned at every possible opportunity'). One-data-core consolidation to replace disparate systems is another stated driver. Formal criteria will crystallise in the RFP primer Zycus is providing and the end-2026 RFP, so shaping that primer is how Zycus sets the criteria.

engagement.top_event: Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) unaddressed and due before BRD fit session on 17 June. These are table stakes for Vendor Selected gate.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

The full shortlist forms only at the end-2026 RFP, it's still Qualified stage (capped), and there's no EB

deal_movement.summary: Live, progressing early-stage shaping deal. Two recorded/known buyer touchpoints in April-May 2026 plus an incoming buyer email 2 Jun and SF activity 23 Jun. Momentum is healthy but the motion is deliberately slow ('long play', RFP end-2026).

items.detail: Implementation resourcing and internal bandwidth flagged as primary blocker. Rachael stated she does not have internal headcount/bandwidth to run a procurement transformation project (2026-05-15 demo per Next Step). Greencore is under 'incredible time pressure' post-acquisition running multiple massive transformations (MBE program: supply chain planning, MES, time/attendance, SAP migration) which may crowd out S2P bandwidth or create change fatigue.

No Economic Buyer mapped and no budget formally approved. Deal is pre-budget approval; Rachael must build business case, present to unnamed senior leadership, and secure funding before RFP can launch. Risk of budget delay or down-scope if business case is not compelling or if synergy priorities shift.

Buyer explicitly stated end-of-2026 RFP timeline and 'this is a long play deal' (2026-04-10 call and Next Step). Close date 2026-04-12 is a placeholder; realistic close is Q1 2027 at earliest. Risk of deal aging out of forecast window or champion cooling if timeline stretches further due to competing transformation priorities.

Competitive shortlist is unknown and will surface at RFP (end-2026). Zycus has shaping position now but could lose influence if competitors build parallel relationships or business case support with unmapped stakeholders (EB, IT, finance) before RFP launch.
Rachael lacks internal headcount/bandwidth to run an implementation amid multiple concurrent MBE transformations (supply chain planning, MES, SAP migration); primary near-term blocker.

She needs Zycus-supplied ammunition and broader threading to convert intent into a funded RFP.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warm: two productive discovery/demo touchpoints (10 Apr, 15 May), an incoming buyer email 2 Jun, and SF activity 23 Jun. Cadence is intentionally slow because the buyer is in a pre-RFP shaping phase, not cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 30 Jun 2026. CloseDate 12 Apr 2026 = -79 days to close (already past). Stage Qualified since CreatedDate 13 Apr 2026 (~11 weeks in stage). Forecast Pipeline. Last verified SF activity 23 Jun 2026 (7 days ago); incoming buyer email 2 Jun 2026 (28 days ago); last buyer call read 10 Apr 2026, on-site demo 15 May 2026 (~6 weeks ago). Buyer explicitly stated RFP towards end of 2026 and 'this is a long play deal', so the close date cannot hold; pace to a credible close is Q1 2027 at the earliest. The deal is genuinely progressing and engaged; the only defect is the date, hence Close Date Risk.
evidence: Rachael Coad, 10 Apr 2026: 'Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026' and Next Step notes 'this is a long play deal - we are keeping them warm until they are ready for RFP' (source: Next Step field + 10 Apr discovery call).
On-site demo held 15 May 2026 with Rachael Coad and James Dean (source: Touchpoint 3 manifest, 15 May 2026).
Incoming buyer email received 2 Jun 2026 and SF LastActivityDate 23 Jun 2026 confirm the deal is live (source: engagement pulse).
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, highly engaged early-stage shaping deal, but the 12 Apr 2026 close date is a placeholder 79 days in the past; the buyer has stated an end-of-2026 RFP and called this a long play, so realistic close is Q1 2027.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (correct category; close date must be reset to ~Q1 2027 to match the buyer-stated end-2026 RFP)

flags: Competitive shortlist is latent but will surface at end-2026 RFP. Rachael confirmed multi-vendor evaluation underway but named no rivals. Zycus is positioned to shape the RFP criteria and business case before competition formally engages, which is a strong influence position. Risk: lose shaping advantage if competitors build relationships or business case support in parallel. Next move: ask Rachael who else is in her pipeline and what concerns or gaps she sees with them, then wedge Merlin AI (she already uses Pactum, proving AI appetite) and SAP partnership as differentiators in the business case.

Champion is single-threaded (Rachael + James only) with no economic buyer mapped: the 8 Jun executive-connect (Anthony Gray to the relevant stakeholder/CPO) is the top lever to unblock the resource/budget concern before competitors engage senior leadership.
Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) are overdue: deliver them now to keep the champion armed for the business case while engagement is warm.

Multi-thread beyond Rachael Coad and James Dean: only 2 buyer-side contacts are on the opportunity; identify and reach the budget approver / senior leadership, IT and finance to de-risk a single-threaded pre-budget deal.
best_practice_check.summary: Strong early-stage shaping position with a self-declared champion; the highest-leverage wins now are arming the champion with a quantified business case, resetting the forecast date, and broadening beyond two contacts before the RFP.

competitive_position.summary: No live S2P rival yet; the shortlist forms at the end-2026 RFP where Zycus is shaping the primer. The strongest current alternative is the fragmented incumbent stack plus point AI tools: Pactum (active, in-production autonomous negotiation tool the buyer praises) and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she loves, alongside Scanmarket (eProcurement incumbent) and DocuSign (contract repository incumbent). The competitive risk is a 'keep best-of-breed / do nothing' path rather than a named platform rival, plus competitors building parallel senior relationships before the RFP.

competitors.name: Pactum (AI autonomous negotiation)
competitors.quote: The AI autonomous negotiation tool, I swear to God, gets mentioned at every possible opportunity.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin autonomous negotiation as native within a unified S2P data core (Tata puts 50% of tail through it) versus a standalone point tool.

competitors.name: Unnamed AI contract-redlining tool
competitors.quote: we've recently got a separate tool for contract redlining AI contract redlining, which I love. It is the best thing ever. But we still don't have a CLM system.
competitors.how_we_win: Position iContract CLM as the system of record the loved redlining tool plugs into or is absorbed by, ending the standalone gap she acknowledged.

competitors.name: Scanmarket (eProcurement incumbent)
competitors.quote: We've got Scanmarket for eProcurement... it's a separate system that doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with eProcurement + guided intake in one platform that ties to contracts and spend, eliminating siloed standalone tools.

competitors.name: DocuSign (contract repository incumbent)
competitors.quote: We're using DocuSign as our repository. DocuSign doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Replace the unconnected repository with iContract CLM integrated to sourcing and supplier records.

competitors.name: Full S2P competitive shortlist (to form at RFP)
competitors.quote: working with you guys and with some of your competitors is being able to create a really compelling business case.
competitors.how_we_win: Use the shaping position now to author the RFP primer and criteria so the eventual RFP reflects Zycus differentiators (agentic single-data-core S2P).

competitors.name: Pactum
competitors.quote: if you'd have asked me 3 years ago, would we have an AI autonomous negotiation tool... we're in our second year of having it now... Pactum
competitors.how_we_win: Show Merlin autonomous negotiation inside one agentic S2P data core (e.g. Tata routing 50% of tail through it) so a standalone point tool becomes redundant.

competitors.name: Scanmarket (incumbent eProcurement)
competitors.quote: we've got Scanmarket for eProcurement... a separate system that doesn't tie into anything
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with one-data-core consolidation that retires disparate standalone systems.

competitors.name: DocuSign (incumbent contract repository)
competitors.quote: We're using Docusign as our repository. Docusign doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate integrated CLM + repository tied to sourcing and spend, removing the disconnected silo.

competitors.name: Full S2P competitive shortlist (to form at end-2026 RFP)
competitors.quote: working with you guys and with some of your competitors is being able to create a really compelling business case
competitors.how_we_win: Own the RFP primer and business case so Zycus sets the evaluation criteria before rivals engage.

This is a long play deal - we are keeping them warm until they are ready for RFP


Conducted on-site discovery and demonstration meeting at the customer's offices on 15th May 2026 with Rachel and James. Rachel showed strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities. James has confirmed attendance at the Zycus procurement event next week — good opportunity to deepen the relationship in a peer environment. Rachel is impressed with what Zycus can deliver but has raised a significant concern around internal resource availability to support a procurement transformation programme. She does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project. This is the primary blocker to near-term progression.

Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026. Zycus is being positioned to help her build the business case and shape the RFP — giving us a strong influence position ahead of formal process launch.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / fragmented incumbent stack (Scanmarket, DocuSign, click) plus full S2P shortlist to form at end-2026 RFP

deltas.subject: Economic Buyer unmapped. Rachael and James are champion and coach but do not control £450K budget approval. Rachael referenced 'conditioning conversations with the powers that be' (supportive but unnamed). Risk: business case stalls at budget gate without exec sponsor. deltas.subject: Implementation resourcing is stated as the primary blocker. Rachael flagged (2026-05-15 demo per Next Step) that she does not have internal headcount/bandwidth to run the project. deltas.subject: Competitive shortlist is latent but will surface at end-2026 RFP. Rachael confirmed multi-vendor evaluation underway but named no rivals. Zycus is positioned to shape the RFP criteria and business case before competition formally engages, which is a strong influence position. Risk: lose shaping advantage if competitors build relationships or business case support in parallel. Next move: ask Rachael who else is in her pipeline and what concerns or gaps she sees with them, then wedge Merlin AI (she already uses Pactum, proving AI appetite) and SAP partnership as differentiators in the business case.

value.name: Pactum
value.quote: if you'd have asked me 3 years ago, would we have an AI autonomous negotiation tool... we're in our second year of having it now... Pactum
value.how_we_win: Show Merlin autonomous negotiation inside one agentic S2P data core (e.g. Tata routing 50% of tail through it) so a standalone point tool becomes redundant.

packets.subject: Pactum

value.name: Scanmarket (incumbent eProcurement)

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain strong and some figures stated (GBP 650m indirect spend, ~30% tail, GBP 80m synergy target) but no Greencore-validated ROI model yet; Zycus owes the savings model (10 Apr 2026).

gaps: Economic buyer / budget approver above Rachael is unnamed in all sources.
Two of three buyer touchpoints (08 Apr, 15 May) were not recorded, so deep content rests on the single 10 Apr discovery call; confidence reflects partial coverage.

Rachel is impressed with what Zycus can deliver but has raised a significant concern around internal resource availability to support a procurement transformation programme. This is the primary blocker to near-term progression.Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026.
